headline: Nvidia-Backed Firmus Hits $5.5B, Signaling Asia AI Infrastructure Race Intensifies
summary: Firmus has raised $1.35B in just six months, reaching a $5.5B valuation as demand for AI data center capacity in Asia accelerates. Nvidia's backing signals a deliberate strategy to seed GPU-dense infrastructure partners outside the hyperscaler ecosystem. This validates that sovereign and regional AI infrastructure is becoming a high-stakes competitive arena, not just a capacity play.
oci_implication: OCI's expansion in Asia-Pacific faces a well-capitalized, Nvidia-aligned rival building purpose-fit AI data centers — potentially capturing enterprise and government deals before OCI can establish regional density. Accelerating OCI region buildout and deepening Nvidia partnership terms in APAC should be a near-term priority to avoid ceding first-mover positioning.
